title: Autonomous Racing Simulation & Digital Twin Framework
tldr: Developed a Unity–ROS2 digital twin for autonomous race cars with scalable cloud deployment, multi-agent AI, and closed-loop vehicle dynamics validation.
category: ros2 unity docker autonomous-vehicles machine-learning digital-twin
contribution: Architected a full-stack autonomous racing simulation framework integrating Unity physics, ROS2 middleware, Dockerized infrastructure, and AI-driven opponent modeling for scalable autonomous vehicle validation.
details: Built a high-fidelity Unity simulator modeling realistic track layouts, tire-road interaction, and vehicle dynamics to evaluate path planning, localization, and control performance.
Containerized ROS2 environments using Docker to enable cloud-based large-scale simulation testing and reproducible deployments.
Developed opponent AI agents for multi-agent racing scenarios to test overtaking, collision avoidance, and competitive decision-making.
Implemented sensor emulation (LiDAR, IMU, odometry) and closed-loop control loops to bridge simulation-to-real autonomy validation.

title: Hybrid Motion Planning with RL-Based Adaptive Control
tldr: Integrated RRT* global planning with DDPG reinforcement learning for dynamic, collision-free robotic navigation.
contribution: Designed a hybrid planning stack combining classical sampling-based optimization with deep reinforcement learning for adaptive local control.
details: Implemented RRT* for optimal global trajectory generation and DDPG for reactive local obstacle avoidance in dynamic environments.
Deployed in PyBullet simulations with static and moving obstacles to validate smoothness, latency, and collision-free navigation.
Engineered reward shaping strategies prioritizing energy efficiency, trajectory smoothness, and safety constraints.
Validated feasibility against kinematic and dynamic constraints of differential drive platforms.

title: 2D SLAM Pipeline for Differential Drive Robot (ROS2)
tldr: Engineered a real-time SLAM pipeline fusing LiDAR and odometry for robust localization and occupancy grid mapping.
contribution: Developed an end-to-end SLAM system integrating probabilistic mapping, sensor fusion, and kinematic modeling.
details: Fused wheel encoder odometry with 2D LiDAR scan matching to generate consistent occupancy grid maps.
Simulated sensor noise and robot kinematics in Gazebo for realistic validation.
Tuned scan alignment and localization parameters to minimize drift and improve map convergence.
Visualized pose estimation and mapping consistency in RViz2 to evaluate robustness.

title: 6-DOF Robotic Arm Simulation & Inverse Kinematics
tldr: Modeled and controlled a 6-DOF manipulator in ROS2 with inverse kinematics and spline-based trajectory planning.
contribution: Built a full robotic arm digital twin implementing URDF/XACRO modeling, IK solvers, and trajectory planning.
details: Modeled articulated joints and actuators using URDF/XACRO for industrial manipulator replication.
Implemented inverse kinematics solvers to convert task-space goals into joint-space commands.
Developed spline-based planners ensuring smooth and collision-free trajectories.
Validated execution in Gazebo and RViz2 to ensure kinematic accuracy and control fidelity.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Pick-and-Place System
tldr: Designed a hardware-integrated robotic system using reinforcement learning for adaptive object manipulation.
contribution: Developed an RL-driven robotic manipulation system integrating mechanical design, embedded control, and wireless communication.
details: Built a robotic pick-and-place platform adaptable to varying object sizes and weights.
Implemented reinforcement learning algorithms to optimize positioning and grasp trajectories.
Developed master-slave WiFi communication between computer and microcontroller for distributed control.
Analyzed and visualized training metrics to refine agent performance and decision-making robustness.

title: Emergent Language Learning in Reinforcement Learning Agents
tldr: Explored multimodal inference and emergent language understanding in RL agents without direct supervision.
contribution: Designed a custom partially observable environment to investigate language skill emergence in RL systems.
details: Built a navigation simulation incorporating indirect language cues encoded as images.
Trained agents to infer navigation goals using multimodal perception.
Evaluated generalization and emergent reasoning capabilities without explicit language supervision.
Demonstrated research-oriented expertise in AI, representation learning, and embodied intelligence.

title: FSAE Brake System Design & CAE Optimization
tldr: Led lightweight brake subsystem development with topology optimization, FEA validation, and manufacturing integration.
contribution: Directed subsystem-level mechanical design from concept to manufacturing for a competition-grade braking system.
details: Designed pedal box, brake pedal, and rotor assemblies using advanced CAD workflows.
Applied structural and thermal FEA in Ansys to validate strength and thermal performance.
Performed MATLAB-based parametric optimization for hydraulic ratios and braking efficiency.
Collaborated with CNC machining, laser cutting, and welding teams to ensure manufacturability.

title: Jet Engine Fuel Pilot Valve Design & Hydraulic Simulation
tldr: Optimized a compact jet engine pilot valve using Siemens NX and Amesim for flow and pressure validation.
contribution: Executed multi-cycle design refinement integrating CAD modeling, GD&T drafting, and hydraulic simulation.
details: Reduced valve size while improving mass flow rate through geometric optimization in Siemens NX.
Performed hydraulic circuit modeling and pressure-flow simulations in Amesim.
Applied GD&T for production-ready part drawings ensuring assembly precision.
Reduced reliance on physical prototyping through simulation-driven validation.

title: Advanced Opponent Modeling for Multi-Agent Autonomous Racing
tldr: Developed AI-driven opponent behavior models to simulate competitive multi-agent autonomous racing environments.
contribution: Engineered competitive AI agents to stress-test autonomy stacks under adversarial and cooperative driving scenarios.
details: Designed behavior models capturing overtaking logic, collision avoidance, and racing line optimization.
Integrated opponent agents into closed-loop simulation for robustness evaluation of planning algorithms.
Analyzed decision-making stability under high-speed, adversarial multi-agent conditions.
Enabled scalable evaluation of autonomous racing strategies in complex competitive environments.
